name: Kane Verify After Task
description: After a spec task completes, decides whether Kane is needed and runs the matching testmd flow if the task changed user-facing browser behavior.

then.prompt:
A spec task just completed. First confirm the app builds and unit tests pass (npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test -- --run). If they fail, fix those first and do NOT run Kane.

If they pass, decide whether this task changed user-facing browser behavior:
- Tasks 4.1, 4.2 (auth UI) → run: kane-cli testmd .testmuai/tests/auth_test.md --agent --headless --timeout 60000
- Tasks 5.1, 5.2, 5.4 (log dialog, feed) → run: kane-cli testmd .testmuai/tests/log_hangout_test.md --agent --headless --timeout 60000
- Tasks 6.1 (leaderboard) → run: kane-cli testmd .testmuai/tests/leaderboard_test.md --agent --headless --timeout 60000
- Tasks 6.3 (profile/badges) → run: kane-cli testmd .testmuai/tests/profile_badges_test.md --agent --headless --timeout 60000
- Tasks 6.5 (skip-a-day/streak UI) → run: kane-cli testmd .testmuai/tests/streak_test.md --agent --headless --timeout 60000
- Tasks 4.3, 5.3, 5.5–5.8, 6.2, 6.4 (unit/property tests only) → skip Kane, say why.

Read the final run_end line from the NDJSON output. If passed, report 'Verified' with the screenshot path. If failed, read the reason and the failing screenshot, fix the app code (NEVER the test), and re-run. Stop after 3 attempts and ask the human.

Never put a raw dollar sign in a Kane objective. Use 'USD' or the number.
